title: Thelypteris dasypoda
taxonomy: Thelypteris dasypoda is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh fern family. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes many species of ferns. At the class level, Thelypteris dasypoda falls under Polypodiopsida, a division of ferns and their allies. intro: Thelypteris dasypoda, also known as the "Southern Maidenhair Fern," is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris dasypoda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thelypteris dasypoda is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin care and wound healing applications.
